entries.d: A woman in stories who does magic
entries.s: The _____ flew across the sky on her broom.

entries.d: The spirit of a dead person that you can sometimes see
entries.s: My little brother is afraid of the _____ in this story.

entries.d: A small container shaped like a pumpkin, used to collect sweets
entries.s: The children carry a _____ to collect sweets.

entries.d: A creature in stories that comes out at night and drinks blood
entries.s: He dressed up as a _____ with a long black cape.

entries.d: Words children say at doors to get sweets on 31 October
entries.s: The kids shouted _____ at every house on the street.

entries.d: A game where you try to catch fruit in water with your mouth
entries.s: We tried to catch apples in water with our teeth — that game is called _____.

entries.d: Something you wear over your face to look different
entries.s: She wore a scary _____ over her face and nobody could see who she was underneath.

entries.d: Something people do every year because they always did it
entries.s: Singing carols at Christmas is a nice _____ that families do every year.

entries.d: To be afraid of something
entries.s: My dog _____ loud noises like thunder — he hides under the bed.

entries.d: To remove a piece of something with a sharp tool
entries.s: Dad _____ the top of the orange vegetable with a big knife.

entries.d: The glass at the front of a house that you look through
entries.s: We put candles on the _____ so people outside could see them.

entries.d: To not give away or throw away
entries.s: Can I _____ this book forever? I don't want to give it back.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with KEEP?

entries.d: Steps inside a building that go up or down
entries.s: I ran up the wooden _____ step by step to my room on the second floor.

entries.d: A small piece of paper with a picture that you can stick on things
entries.s: I put a star _____ on my notebook.

entries.d: Small sugary foods like chocolate or gummy bears
entries.s: I ate too many _____ and now my tummy hurts.

entries.d: A sharp tool you use to cut food
entries.s: Be careful with that _____! It is very sharp.

entries.d: A hundred years
entries.s: We live in the 21st _____. One hundred years ago, people had no phones.

entries.d: Special clothes you wear to look like someone else
entries.s: She wore a cat _____ with ears and a tail.

entries.d: Was not able to
entries.s: I searched everywhere but I _____ find my keys this morning.

entries.d: Pretty and lovely to look at
entries.s: Look at that tiny puppy! It is so _____ with its small paws and floppy ears.

entries.d: A piece of clothing for girls that covers the body and legs
entries.s: She wore a red _____ to the school dance.

entries.d: A place where dead people are put under the ground
entries.s: The old _____ full of tombstones next to the church looks spooky at night.

entries.d: A word for 'me' when you do something alone
entries.s: I made this birthday card all by _____.

entries.d: A word to ask about what to do next
entries.s: _____ we go to the park or stay at home?

entries.d: Not feeling well in your body
entries.s: I feel _____ today. I think I ate something bad and now my stomach hurts.

entries.d: A strong woman in a story who saves people
entries.s: My favourite _____ is a brave woman who saves people and can fly.

entries.d: Not calm or gentle; full of energy
entries.s: The children were very _____ at the party, jumping everywhere and shouting loudly.

entries.d: To make someone feel afraid
entries.s: My brother likes to hide behind doors and _____ me in the dark.

entries.d: A hard hat you wear on your head when riding a bike
entries.s: Always wear your _____ when you ride your bike.

entries.d: A friendly word for a group of people
entries.s: Hey _____ — Tom, Max, and Jack — do you want to play football after school?

entries.d: To not have something any more because you cannot find it
entries.s: Don't _____ your phone again and leave it somewhere by mistake! Keep it safe in your bag.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with LOSE?
